topic.what: Detection of model substitution attacks in hosted LLM services where providers serve cheaper models than advertised
topic.how: Pre-query Merkle commitment to SAE feature traces at a published probe layer with z-score verification against a public probe library
topic.why_matters: Providers have financial incentive to substitute models; prior work (SVIP) has a parallel-serve side-channel that allows evasive substitution

characteristics.what_it_measures: True positive rate across 17 attacker strategies
characteristics.vs_baseline: All 17 attackers (same-family lifts, cross-family substitutes, rank≤128 LoRA) detected at scale-stable thresholds
characteristics.context: 100% detection of 17 attackers spanning weak-to-strong ladder including parallel-serve baseline evaders

characteristics.what_itasures: Ratio of detection z-score to threshold
characteristics.vs_baseline: Strongest black-box attacker 2.78× threshold on Qwen3
characteristics.context: Margin grows with SAE width; white-box E2E attacker (z=4.99) exceeds black-box optimum (4.19)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Type I error under honest serving
characteristics.context: Per-session FPR bounded by kα under independence; within-session ρ=0.88 empirically deflates FPR

characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline: honest forward pass without commitment
characteristics.context: Drops from 18.5% at B=1 to ≤2.1% at B=32; parallel-commit is 0.97× honest wall-clock (∼3% gap)

characteristics.evidence: Table 2, Merkle root SHA-256 32 bytes + top-k indices/values
characteristics.context: Merkle leaf = (x, y, mid, t, tok32t)

characteristics.evidence: Section 5.5, same |P|=96 library at L=20 width-131k
characteristics.context: Scale-stable detection threshold enables single deployment configuration
